title: Series Index
surface: v2 series-index — catalog of all multi-part syllabi
summary: List of ALL series, lifted from v1's tag-only treatment to a primary nav-level surface. Three series-card variants: flat parts-list (compact), chapter-grid (visually rich), progress-bar (in-progress series).

strategy.decision: Promote series from buried-tag to primary nav surface. Each series gets a card with category + part-count + total-time + syllabus quote + parts list (or chapter grid or progress bar, depending on variant).
strategy.why: Publication archetype Lesson 4: 'Series and counterpoints are architectural, not chrome. v1 publications under-use these two formats because they're invisible in the IA.' Stitch's SeriesIndex.tsx reveals series as one of the primary surfaces — building this is consistent with that elevation.
strategy.shipped: v1 has no equivalent. Series is just a tag in v1's tag system; readers can't see a series catalog or progress through one.
strategy.gap: v2 adds a dedicated /series-index route with three card variants. Three series included: Inference Era (flat parts-list), Senior IC Track (chapter grid), Distributed Consensus (progress bar).
strategy.question: What's the right syllabus copy register for each series? Stitch's were academic-flavored; voice-guide drift risk. The placeholder syllabus quotes need voice-scoring.

inheritsFromV1: all 8 of v1's content collections are still available
breaksFromV1: Series elevated from tag to primary nav-level surface
Three series-card layout variants (flat / chapter-grid / progress)
decisionDependencies: D3 (format mix — confirms series stays as primary surface)

currentState.summary: v1 series is a tag-filtered list with no progress, no card variants, no parts list.
currentState.annotation: v2 promotes series from tag to dedicated route + adds card variants per Stitch SeriesIndex pattern.
